calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-29-2026 Continued To Sep-22-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Relief From Scriveners Error And Request For Entry Of Default
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA INSURANCE GUARANTEE ASSOCIATION's, AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO GATEWAY INSURANCE COMPANY, IN LIQUIDATION, Motion For Relief From Scriveners Error And Request For Entry Of Default.

The unopposed motion for entry of default is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. The omission of the descriptor "as successor in interest to Gateway Insurance Company, in liquidation" from the summons does not defeat notice where plaintiff served the complaint with the summons. "[T]he provisions of the [Code of Civil Procedure] and all proceedings under it are to be liberally construed, with a view to effect its objects and to promote justice." (Hammer Collections Co. v. Ironsides Computer Corp. (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 899, 902 [citation simplified].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
